items.title: Iran says closed Strait of Hormuz as US deal hits obstacle ahead of Swiss talks
items.description: Iran said Saturday it had closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israel’s military campaign in Lebanon, while warning that upcoming talks with the United States in Switzerland are unlikely to advance unless the fighting stops. President Donald Trump countered by threatening U.S.-imposed tolls on shipping through the strategic waterway if a final agreement is not reached within 60 days.

items.title: Malaysia’s 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders explores how faith can empower the youth
items.description: Religious leaders, scholars and students gathered in Kuala Lumpur for the Third International Summit of Religious Leaders, focusing on empowering youth in an era of rapid technological and social change.

items.title: Rheinmetall to start talks on Japan defence production, Nikkei reports
New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
items.description: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

items.title: From the sublime to the ridiculous
items.description: In the past, countless ministers have been sacrificed to protect a President but now presidential popularity is being laid on the line to save a minister. Leer más

items.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
items.description: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

items.title: In Nepal’s highlands, climate change threatens Tibet’s Bon faith
items.description: In Lubra, Nepal’s last Bon village, climate-driven floods are destroying homes and land, endangering ancient traditions.

items.title: 'I buried my parents one day after the other'
items.description: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.
items.source: Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely

items.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
items.description: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones were launched towards an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

items.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
items.description: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."

items.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
items.description: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

items.title: Germany news: Summer thunderstorms wreak havoc
items.description: Germany was lashed overnight by summer storms that caused flooding, injuries and evacuations. Lightning, high winds and heavy rain saw several people hospitalized. DW has the latest.

items.title: Are UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s political days numbered?
items.description: British PM resisting intensifying party pressure to step aside.

items.title: Russia, Madagascar foreign ministers hold talks in Moscow
items.description: Russia's Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov welcomed his Madagascar counterpart, Alice N'Diaye, in Moscow on Friday

items.title: Heatwave nears 40C in France, forcing tourists to change routines
items.description: France is enduring a widespread heatwave, with temperatures nearing 40C in parts of the country and forcing tourists and residents to change their routines.

items.title: Farewell, Scotland: Boston won’t forget the Tartan Army
items.description: They arrived with kilts, songs and endless optimism. They leave behind unforgettable memories. For one week, Boston belonged to the Tartan Army. As Scotland's supporters head to Miami, their next World Cup destination, they say goodbye to a city that embraced them from day one.

items.title: Magic Messi bags hat-trick, breaks World Cup record as Argentina rout Algeria 3-0
items.description: Lionel Messi opened his record-breaking sixth World Cup with a hat-trick to become the tournament's joint all-time top scorer on Tuesday as holders Argentina launched their bid for back-to-back titles with a dazzling 3-0 rout of Algeria. Leer más

items.title: Japan, South Korea stocks hit records as oil prices fall
items.description: Oil tankers have started sailing through the Strait of Hormuz after the United States lifted its blockade on Iran on Thursday as an interim deal to end the three-month war took effect.

items.title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
items.description: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

items.title: Baerbock: 'Germany team is an example to Germany's kids'
items.description: The president of the UN General Assembly, Annalena Baerbock, praises the diversity of Germany's football team as it plays in the World Cup.

items.title: Iran warns of ‘reciprocal action’ if US doesn’t honour MOU commitments
items.description: Iran warns of ‘reciprocal action’ if US doesn’t honour MOU commitments.

items.title: Canadian beer prices test German fans' thirst at FIFA World Cup
items.description: The FIFA World Cup is in full swing, including in Canada. Toronto is hosting Saturday's match between Germany and the Ivory Coast. The German supporters are there and they’re angry – according to them, the price of beer is far too high. Story by Daniel Quinlan and Antoine Fenaux.

items.title: Yen at brink of 40-year low puts markets on intervention watch
War helped 're-enliven' Iran's regime. Trump's truce may complicate that
items.description: For decades the Islamic republic has been using its "main enemy" America to distract from its repressive regime, analysts say. But the recent truce with Donald Trump could change that.

items.title: Germany's Left Party is dreaming big
items.description: The socialist Left Party has seen its membership soar and is especially popular with young female voters. Could it soon come to power in the city state of Berlin?

items.title: India's Nifty IT index at three-year low as bellwether Accenture flags weak outlook
Trump insists Italy’s Meloni sought photos with him to boost popularity
items.description: Comments escalate a row between the once-close leaders that has emerged during the conflict with Iran.

items.title: Ebola: Health workers battle virus and stigma
items.description: On the front lines of the Ebola outbreak, healthcare workers are exposed not only to Ebola in health centers but also to rejection by their communities

items.title: Brazil and Haiti fans celebrate together after World Cup match
items.description: Fans of Brazil and Haiti’s national soccer teams celebrated together as they left Lincoln Financial Field in Philadelphia on Friday evening.

items.title: In Trump's shadow, Vance becomes face of Iran deal
items.description: His fierce defence of the Iran plan amid mounting criticism comes as speculation intensifies about a possible 2028 presidential run.

items.title: NGOs in Lebanon denounce Israeli attacks on medics amid ceasefire
items.description: On March 2nd, Hezbollah dragged Lebanon into the war when it launched rockets at Israel in what it said was revenge for the killing of Iran’s supreme leader. Since then, Israel's campaign has killed more than 3,900 people and displaced 20% of the population. The humanitarian situation on the ground is dire. Details from the ground with Dr Mina Naguib, an emergency physician with Doctors Without Borders in Beirut.

items.title: Milei shows support for under-fire Adorni at Flag Day rally
items.description: President stands by close ally Manuel Adorni despite mounting pressure over undeclared assets and growing calls for his resignation. Leer más

items.title: Israeli Minister says 'all of Lebanon must burn' after four soldiers killed
items.description: With all due respect to the Americans, Israel must make it clear to the entire world that the blood of our sons and the security of our citizens are not up for bargaining. All of Lebanon must burn, says Far-right Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir

items.title: 'Yanks flying up in the air': The World War II brawl that consumed Brisbane
items.description: The American war effort made Brisbane its stronghold in 1942, and thousands of attractive American soldiers were situated in Brisbane. That didn't sit so well with the Australians.

items.title: Trial into disappearance of missing five-year-old Loan begins
items.description: Fate of five-year-old Loan Danilo Peña remains one of Argentina’s most haunting mysteries of recent years; Long-awaited trial due to begin, days after the second anniversary of child’s disappearance. Leer más

items.title: South Sudan repatriates Vietnamese man deported from US
items.description: A Vietnamese national deported to South Sudan by the Trump administration under its controversial third-country deportation program was repatriated to Vietnam on Friday after spending more than a year in detention

items.title: After a year of Cristina Fernández de Kirchner's house arrest, the fallout endures
items.description: A year on from the court ruling that confirmed her sentence, Cristina Fernández de Kirchner remains under house arrest and barred from holding public office. A look at status of the other court cases facing the former president and the ongoing dispute over the conduct of judges and prosecutors. Leer más

items.title: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved
items.description: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

items.title: African startups showcase practical innovations at Viva Tech conference
items.description: At VivaTech, fintech, banking solutions and AI have a central place in the startup ecosystem. But beyond these trends, some projects are focusing on very practical issues, such as reducing post-harvest losses in Africa — a key challenge for food security and agricultural development.

items.title: Brent set for 8% weekly fall as Israel, Hezbollah agree ceasefire
Zelenskyy returns Poland's highest honor as row deepens
items.description: Ukraine's president has returned a medal to Poland after a decision to strip him of the honor. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia's war in Ukraine.

items.title: World Cup awaits Argentina's Julián Álvarez, the striker worth – at least – 150 million euros
items.description: Real Madrid and Barcelona are both chasing the Argentine forward this transfer window, but Atlético Madrid insists its star striker isn't going anywhere. Leer más

items.title: British man dies in paragliding accident in Spain
items.description: Authorities in Catalonia confirmed that the 63-year-old died in the Palau de Noguera area on Wednesday.

items.title: World Cup blues in Messi and Milei’s Argentina
items.description: The World Cup provides partial reprieve for nearly six weeks, especially if Messi & Co are in it for a deep run. Politics, though, continues its course, and Adorni is coming up to the chopping block. Leer más

items.title: Labour's Andy Burnham wins a special election, setting up a showdown with Starmer to lead Britain
items.description: Labour's Andy Burnham, the current mayor of Greater Manchester, has won a special election for a seat in Parliament that puts him in a position to challenge embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer for leadership of the country.

items.title: Bolivia's Paz declares state of emergency over blockades
items.description: President Rodrigo Paz says he has declared the state of emergency "to free the country's roads." Blockades have become a major tactic in weeks of intensifying protests to demand that the president resign.

items.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system
items.description: Children's paths through Germany's school system are often determined before their first day of kindergarten, according to the national report on education. An elementary school in Bonn is offering a new model for kids.

items.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
items.description: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

items.title: Is the most expensive World Cup in history worth the cost for travelling fans?
items.description: There has been endless talk about fans paying high prices for this World Cup. So is it worth it? And is it time to accept the people's game is no longer within everyone's reach?

items.title: Deep-sea denizens go years without food with clever biological fix
Steve Rosenberg: Moscow oil refinery attack brings Russia's war with Ukraine closer to home
items.description: There are moments when life in Moscow feels completely normal - Thursday morning wasn't one of them, writes Steve Rosenberg.

items.title: Ivory Coast police arrest man over murky Abidjan homes demolition
items.description: Ivory Coast police said Friday they had arrested a man accused of using a fake court document to order the demolition of a large area of an Abidjan neighbourhood.

items.title: Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi to face rape trial, French court rules
items.description: A French appeals court on Friday confirmed that Moroccan football star Achraf Hakimi will face trial for the alleged rape of a woman in 2023, which the Paris Saint-Germain defender denies.

items.title: 'They came with machetes'
items.description: Protesters have set 30 June as the date for all undocumented migrants to leave the country.
items.source: deadline looms for migrants to leave South Africa

items.title: Czech government causes alarm with plan to scrap license fee
items.description: The Czech government has approved a bill to put the funding of public service media under direct political control. Critics warn that if passed, the bill would put a pillar of Czech democracy at risk.

items.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz ‘closed’ as Israeli strikes on Lebanon continue
items.description: Pakistan, which mediated the U.S.-Iran ceasefire, said “technical talks” between the two sides were set to restart in Geneva on Sunday (June 21, 2026)

items.title: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad built on African roots
items.description: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad has leaders with African heritage. The diversity of the team is a strength.

items.title: What you need to know about the preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement signed by Trump
items.description: Here's a look at the preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran, and the challenges that remain to find lasting peace.

items.title: ICC chief prosecutor Karim Khan suspended by British regulator
items.description: Khan had already been suspended by the ICC's governing body over allegations of sexual misconduct with a female aide, in a scandal that has dragged on for more than two years

items.title: Humanoid robots and smart homes put AI centre stage at VivaTech 2026 in Paris
items.description: France is putting artificial intelligence and robotics in the spotlight at VivaTech 2026, which opened on 17 June at Paris Expo Porte de Versailles.
